mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor markets, which drives corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly higher during periods of UNRATE decline below 4.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production growth combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) indicates robust supply-side dynamics, creating a positive feedback loop for equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly negative during periods of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 2.0% signals strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly higher during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.0% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts (HOUST > 1200) indicates strong consumer confidence and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly higher during periods of HOUST > 1200 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150k) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 5.0%) signals a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) during a recessionary regime indicates supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly negative during periods of INDPRO decline < 100 (t-test p > 0.05).
